scores.correctness_notes: All core required elements are present and correct: services with override/command/startup/environment, HTTP checks with correct fields, log-targets with loki type and correct location format, on-check-failure linking checks to restart action. Structure matches gold standard spec.
scores.hallucination_notes: The 'requires' and 'after' fields under services appear to be hallucinated — Pebble services use 'requires' for dependencies but the 'after' field for ordering is not a standard Pebble layer spec field. Also 'description' at the top-level layer and per-service level, 'working-dir', 'user', 'group', 'kill-delay', 'backoff-delay', 'backoff-factor', 'backoff-limit', 'on-failure' are valid Pebble fields per the actual spec, so those are fine. The 'after' field specifically is questionable but minor.
